tree.setup: A lazy Sunday afternoon. Your friend Xiao Chen hasn't been seen in three weeks — no messages, no calls, nothing. This is unusual for someone who normally sends you food photos at least twice a day. You decide to call and check in, half expecting to discover he's either fallen in love, started a business, or both.
tree.setup_zh: 一个慵懒的星期天下午。你的朋友小陈已经三个星期没有消息了——没有微信，没有电话，什么都没有。对于一个平时至少每天给你发两次美食照片的人来说，这很不正常。你决定打个电话看看，半猜他不是恋爱了就是创业了，或者两个都有。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 喂？哎，好久没联系了！最近忙什么呢？
turns.text_pinyin: wèi? āi, hǎo jiǔ méi liánxì le! zuìjìn máng shénme ne?
turns.text_en: Hello? Hey, long time no contact! What've you been busy with?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Ask what he's been up to.

options.text_zh: 就是啊！三个星期都没有你的消息。你没事吧？是不是发生了什么？
options.text_pinyin: jiùshì a! sān gè xīngqī dōu méiyǒu nǐ de xiāoxi. nǐ méi shì ba? shìbúshì fāshēng le shénme?
options.text_en: Exactly! Three weeks without a word from you. Are you OK? Did something happen?
options.feedback: Concerned but not intrusive — the tone lands perfectly. Xiao Chen laughs, which means the news is probably good.

options.text_pinyin: nǐ zěnme bù huí wǒ xiāoxi a?
options.text_en: Why didn't you reply to my messages?
options.feedback: A bit accusatory but fair. He owed you at least a food photo.
Classic opener but lacks the warmth of actual concern. He'll fill in the blanks himself.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 哈哈，我没事！其实是我换了工作，刚开始特别忙，每天加班到很晚。现在好一点了。你最近怎么样？
turns.text_pinyin: hāhā, wǒ méi shì! qíshí shì wǒ huàn le gōngzuò, gāng kāishǐ tèbié máng, měi tiān jiā bān dào hěn wǎn. xiànzài hǎo yìdiǎn le. nǐ zuìjìn zěnmeyàng?
turns.text_en: Haha, I'm fine! Actually I changed jobs — at first it was super busy, overtime every day until late. It's better now. How about you?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Share your news and suggest meeting up.

options.text_zh: 换工作了？恭喜啊！我这边还是老样子。周末有空吗？出来吃个饭，你给我讲讲新工作。
options.text_pinyin: huàn gōngzuò le? gōngxǐ a! wǒ zhèbiān háishì lǎo yàngzi. zhōumò yǒu kòng ma? chūlái chī gè fàn, nǐ gěi wǒ jiǎngjiǎng xīn gōngzuò.
options.text_en: Changed jobs? Congratulations! Same old for me. Free this weekend? Let's grab a meal — tell me about the new job.
options.feedback: Congratulations plus a meal invite — the friendship maintenance manual. Xiao Chen is already thinking about restaurants.

options.text_zh: 我挺好的。周末见个面吧？
options.text_pinyin: wǒ tǐng hǎo de. zhōumò jiàn gè miàn ba?
options.text_en: I'm good. Meet up this weekend?

options.feedback: The conversational equivalent of a shrug. Xiao Chen waits for more that doesn't come.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 好啊！周六中午怎么样？我知道一家新开的湖南菜馆，评价特别好。
turns.text_pinyin: hǎo a! zhōuliù zhōngwǔ zěnmeyàng? wǒ zhīdào yì jiā xīn kāi de Húnán cài guǎn, píngjià tèbié hǎo.
turns.text_en: Sure! How about Saturday noon? I know a new Hunan restaurant that has great reviews.

options.text_zh: 周六中午可以！湖南菜我喜欢，不过别太辣的啊。老地方见还是你发个地址给我？
options.text_pinyin: zhōuliù zhōngwǔ kěyǐ! Húnán cài wǒ xǐhuān, búguò bié tài là de a. lǎo dìfāng jiàn háishì nǐ fā gè dìzhǐ gěi wǒ?
options.text_en: Saturday noon works! I love Hunan food, but not too spicy though. Meet at the usual spot or send me the address?
options.feedback: Confirming, setting a spice preference, and arranging logistics — the complete response. The food photos will resume soon.
Simple and happy. He'll text you the address.

options.text_pinyin: Húnán cài tài là le ba……
options.feedback: Xiao Chen sighs. He's been saving this restaurant recommendation for three weeks.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 放心，我们可以点不辣的。我把地址微信发给你。对了，你还是那个手机号吧？
turns.text_pinyin: fàngxīn, wǒmen kěyǐ diǎn bú là de. wǒ bǎ dìzhǐ Wēixìn fā gěi nǐ. duìle, nǐ háishì nàge shǒujī hào ba?
turns.text_en: Don't worry, we can order mild dishes. I'll WeChat you the address. By the way, you still have the same phone number?

options.text_zh: 对，还是这个号。那周六见！对了，这次该你请客了吧？
options.text_pinyin: duì, háishì zhège hào. nà zhōuliù jiàn! duìle, zhè cì gāi nǐ qǐngkè le ba?
options.text_en: Yep, same number. See you Saturday! By the way, it's your turn to treat, right?
options.feedback: The eternal Chinese friendship question: whose turn to pay? Xiao Chen groans, but you can hear the grin.

options.text_pinyin: duì. zhōuliù jiàn, bié zài xiāoshī le!
options.text_en: Yes. See you Saturday — don't disappear again!
options.feedback: A gentle warning wrapped in friendship. He promises not to.
Quick sign-off. The food photos will return by Wednesday — that's the real sign of reconciliation.

tree.cultural_note: In Chinese friendships, 好久不见 (hǎo jiǔ bújiàn, 'long time no see') is always followed by a meal plan. The question of 谁请客 (shéi qǐngkè, 'who treats') is a core social ritual — friends keep an informal running tally.
